tweets.text: The United States and Singapore work together for greater impact in the region and across the globe. As a founding member of ASEAN and a champion of a stable and open international system, Singapore is a key U.S. partner in the Indo-Pacific.

.@SecBlinken is in Singapore today. The Secretary will engage with Singaporean government officials and civil society, addressing topics ranging from critical and emerging technologies to climate change. https://t.co/QAdbH4xK2J

Need a quick explainer on @ASEAN? @USMission2ASEAN Ambassador Yohannes Abraham breaks down how member countries and the U.S. are advancing a shared vision for the region. And he even takes on the impossible task of defining ASEAN’s work in a single word. https://t.co/CcQ4zVs4o4
.@SecBlinken participates in a conversation on Advancing Security and Prosperity in the Indo-Pacific, in partnership with @NUSingapore's @LKYSch. https://t.co/LoEkD2ngJZ

Each year as we roll out our Trafficking in Persons Report, we recognizes individuals dedicated to helping end human trafficking. On #WorldDayAgainstTraffickinginPersons we spotlight this year’s 10 heroes, all working in their communities to confront this global challenge. https://t.co/ROG6SSwbeT

.@SecBlinken is in Manila today. With the U.S.-Philippine Alliance stronger than ever, we’re deepening our economic partnerships and building upon our vibrant people-to-people ties. #FriendsPartnersAllies #PartnersInProsperity 🇺🇸🇵🇭 https://t.co/FcrEWHqz4D

.@SecBlinken participates in a Joint Press Availability with @SecDef Austin, Filipino Secretary of Foreign Affairs Manalo, and Secretary of National Defense Teodoro from Manila. https://t.co/3IlH0Jz9gh

The United States and the Philippines have the longest continuously running Fulbright program in the world. The Philippines is home to more than 9,000 Filipino alumni of U.S. exchange programs and 1,000 U.S. citizens have participated in exchange programs in the Philippines. https://t.co/Y49ua3B0W5
Earlier today, @SecBlinken delivered remarks to the press from the Quad ministerial meeting in Tokyo. https://t.co/X1MC0DQfTE

The Quad 🇦🇺🇯🇵🇮🇳🇺🇸 is one of the most important diplomatic partnerships you probably haven't heard of.

Our Ambassadors @USAmbIndia Eric Garcetti, @USEmbAustralia Caroline Kennedy, and @USAmbJapan Rahm Emanuel break it down for you. https://t.co/gEHsmGqg1C

This week at State, we host #AGOA2024, welcome Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu to D.C., the #ParisOlympics open with @FLOTUS cheering on #TeamUSA, and Secretary Blinken travels to the Indo-Pacific with stops in Laos and Vietnam. https://t.co/hqjwEz5tIk
Earlier today .@SecBlinken participated in a 2+2 joint press availability from Tokyo. https://t.co/AZUwnU2B1x

Secretary Blinken has arrived in Tokyo. While in Japan, he will join @SecDef Austin, Foreign Minister Kamikawa, and Defense Minister Kihara for a 2+2 Ministerial Meeting and also engage with his Quad counterparts. https://t.co/TrvBKuCeEw

A range of educational and cultural programs that span nearly seven decades have enhanced people-to-people ties between the U.S. and Laos. In fact, the American Center in Vientiane offers over 730 free programs to Lao youth and attracted 15,000 visitors last year. https://t.co/sajqFu4rao

.@SecBlinken is in Vientiane, capital of Laos, to take part in @ASEAN meetings and the East Asia Summit Foreign Ministers’ Meeting. The U.S. will keep working with and through ASEAN to tackle the biggest challenges, while seizing on what we see as almost limitless opportunities. https://t.co/GxGIyEOoPN
The Olympic Games are here! We asked a few of our #TeamUSA athletes what they are looking forward to most outside of competing in their sports. It was croissants for the win. https://t.co/C6bvdHr3Ry

Today, we celebrate the 34th anniversary of the Americans with Disabilities Act and #DisabilityPrideMonth! The talents and skills of our colleagues with disabilities makes U.S. foreign policy stronger, smarter, and more creative every day. https://t.co/KDdavzMSIF

As we mark the 34th anniversary of the Americans with Disabilities Act, we highlight how the law has led to a more equitable, accessible, and inclusive world. Explore our interactive story for more. https://t.co/LvGCEHq2uM

It’s #NationalInternDay! 🎉 Curious about what our interns are up to this summer? We asked some of them to share their experiences.

If you’re inspired to join us next season, check https://t.co/CqRHMQaeoc for more! https://t.co/NSvpfcTgzr

The African Growth and Opportunity Act has let entrepreneurs expand to new markets to grow their businesses. It’s created tens of thousands of good jobs across our nations – from Portland, Oregon to Port Louis, Mauritius. — @SecBlinken #AGOA2024 https://t.co/8kjNCVPvS2
Learn more from @POTUS about our economic partnerships with African nations through AGOA : https://t.co/O9Ex9PJftL https://t.co/HWqRq3VwJJ

AGOA is not just about generating more opportunities to trade with the U.S. We want to create better opportunities that benefit businesses in all our countries, support our workers, and protect our environment. https://t.co/btdG1BVDGD
As @POTUS said when he hosted the U.S.-Africa Leaders Summit: “When Africa succeeds, the United States succeeds… [and] the whole world succeeds.”

Through the African Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA), the United States is investing in Africa’s economic growth, bringing quality products to U.S. shelves and spurring job growth on both sides of the Atlantic. And it’s delivering success for both U.S. and African communities and businesses. 🧵

The U.S. and our @G7 partners want people everywhere to thrive. That means helping those in emerging markets develop what they need to participate in the global economy. And its why the G7 launched the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment. https://t.co/ljfNaeZIfI
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the African Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA) Private Sector Forum at the @USIP. https://t.co/vHrvilFuLK
We are thrilled that the Winter Olympics will be returning to Salt Lake City, Utah in 2034. Congrats  @SLC2030, @SLCmayor , @UtahGov, and @TeamUSA! #SLCUT2034 https://t.co/ZHkJdG2Imm
What’s #AmericasPartnership for Economic Prosperity? It does a lot to keep the Americas competitive in business and commerce. We explain how — in 30 seconds. https://t.co/M7kJ3LQoSC
The United States remains committed to improving the lives of the Haitian people, with the Transitional Presidential Council playing a crucial role in paving the way for free and fair elections.

This week at State, Secretary Blinken hosts the #AmericasPartnership ministerial, focused on strengthening Western Hemisphere economies. The Secretary also attends the @AspenSecurity Forum, participating in a fireside chat on foreign policy. https://t.co/SIwjgGrc4g
From baseball to brisket, NATO Day at @Nationals Park was a home run. #NATOSummit ⚾️ 🏟 https://t.co/AMrEnNDOjH

We’re in a new era of space exploration — together. Through the Artemis Accords, the State Department and @NASA are bringing nations together with a common vision for peaceful, sustainable, and transparent cooperation in space. 🧑🏼‍🚀 https://t.co/FmOYL3Emkd
The hallmark of the United States is how we openly and transparently confront our challenges and differences. https://t.co/YejcDwuyFO
.@SecBlinken participates in an @AspenSecurity Forum fireside chat, moderated by National Public Radio’s Mary Louise Kelly, in Aspen, Colorado. https://t.co/fvVpy5cnDb
All 32 NATO Allies gathered for the #NATOSummit in Washington last week. Here’s a look back. #1NATO75Years https://t.co/Rj1f5kOgGO
Actions that undermine stability in the West Bank, including attacks by Israeli settlers against Palestinians and Palestinian attacks against Israelis, are unacceptable. https://t.co/wz78VveCLN

Every year, we publish a report to help American businesses make informed decisions on where to invest worldwide, spurring economic growth at home and abroad. Take a look at the latest report prepared by our economic officers stationed around the world.
https://t.co/1NUonzZK0o
Through the Americas Partnership for Economic Prosperity, we’re working together to create economies that work for everyone. 🇧🇧 🇨🇦 🇨🇱 🇨🇴 🇨🇷 🇩🇴 🇪🇨 🇲🇽 🇵🇦 🇵🇪 🇺🇾 🇺🇸 https://t.co/L4ibQSp4rx

.@SecBlinken hosts an #AmericasPartnership for Economic Prosperity Ministerial meeting, focused on inclusive growth in the Western Hemisphere and the region’s global competitiveness. https://t.co/qiHTE2opBR

This week at State, the #NATOSummit in Washington brings together 32 Allies, focused on providing support to Ukraine and keeping our 1 billion citizens safe and secure. @USNATO’s Christine Getzler Vaughan breaks it down. #1NATO75Years https://t.co/TvDbxzowkt

75 years. 32 Allies. 1 Billion people protected.

Born from the ashes of WWII, @NATO is a beacon of peace and stability. #StrongerTogether https://t.co/sGXb91jJ15
It's a pivotal moment for the world. We stand behind our shared vision of a peaceful and prosperous transatlantic community today and well into the future. – @POTUS https://t.co/4lk5pmug7i

And that's a wrap on this year's #NATOSummit in Washington, DC! We were honored to host our @NATO Allies and celebrate 75 years of the most powerful defensive alliance in the world.

Next stop: Netherlands 2025. https://t.co/nVieUnSYZK

At the @NATO Summit, the Alliance called on China to uphold the purposes and principles of the UN Charter and to cease all material and political support to Russia’s war effort. https://t.co/CzoFzglkhO

How it started → How it’s going

1949: Secretary Acheson, with President Truman behind him, signs the North Atlantic Treaty alongside 12 other nations → 75 years later: @POTUS Biden and @SecBlinken celebrate the Alliance’s successes alongside @NATO’s 32 member nations. https://t.co/vigvwSHaqM

I believe the American people understand that America is stronger, stronger because of our Alliances. I believe the American consensus from Truman to Reagan to me still holds today: America cannot retreat from the world. — @POTUS at @NATO Summit https://t.co/cYD9X5D6Pa
The NATO Alliance thrives because we've made hard choices that meet the tests of time. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/N3v6IACdoE

At this week’s @NATO Washington Summit, we’re taking tangible steps to increase NATO’s role in building a capable Ukrainian force, supporting its ongoing reforms, better integrating Ukraine into the Alliance. https://t.co/rSnfpY0c36

Stronger than Ever - Sweden is the newest member of NATO, joining in March 2024. “Sweden’s membership will certainly make Sweden safer and our Alliance stronger and even more secure,” said Julianne Smith, the U.S. Ambassador to NATO. https://t.co/Mcu70YlTPv

Brave New Ally – Following centuries of military neutrality, Finland joined NATO in 2023 as the organization’s 31st Ally following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. President Biden called Finland’s accession “the fastest ratification process in NATO’s modern history.” https://t.co/jF83kZspEm

The Power of Unity - Soldiers from Montenegro hold the national flag during a 2017 ceremony in Brussels to mark Montenegro’s entry into NATO. “Montenegro is a highly valued NATO Ally,” said Secretary General Jens Stoltenberg. https://t.co/5Ip8ogbFG8

Soldiers aboard the USS Eisenhower in 2009 spell out NATO and OTAN, the French language acronym. France served as NATO headquarters for 15 years shortly after the Alliance’s founding. https://t.co/ZnmifbWVbl

March 29, 2004: Bulgaria, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia and Slovenia become members of NATO. With their decision to join, the Alliance saw its biggest enlargement since its founding and clearly demonstrated the Alliance’s commitment to shared democratic values. https://t.co/mho4F5Vvb6

Flight for Peace - Soldiers board a plane bound for Kosovo in 2004 as part of the NATO-led Kosovo Force (KFOR), a peace-support operation that continues today to maintain a safe environment and freedom of movement for all communities living in Kosovo. https://t.co/PAARrLXyDg

An Anniversary of Enlargement - NATO celebrated its 50th anniversary in 1999 as Czechia, Poland, and Hungary joined the Alliance. Then Secretary of State Albright meets with foreign ministers of the three nations during a ceremony recognizing their accession in Independence, Missouri, President Truman’s hometown.

Celebrating Unity - Chefs carry a NATO ice sculpture with sparklers into a formal dinner during the ministerial meeting in Copenhagen, Denmark in May 1958. Denmark had maintained a neutral policy for decades before becoming a founding NATO member in 1949. https://t.co/hoW2vZ8Whm

Born in Freedom - Just like NATO, West Germany was established in 1949. After joining the Alliance in 1955, the nation hosted troops to deter aggression from the East and ultimately reunified with East Germany in 1990. Konrad Adenauer, the nation’s first chancellor, was instrumental in membership negotiations.

Peace, Not War - President Eisenhower inspects the Supreme Headquarters Allied Powers Europe flag near Paris in 1951. “In all history, this is the first time that an allied headquarters has been set up in peace, to preserve the peace and not to wage war,” he said. https://t.co/Zn9GCUnoOM

How it All Began - Secretary of State Dean Acheson made our alliance official in 1949 when he signed the North Atlantic Treaty with President Truman and Vice President Barkley at his side. The U.S. is one of 12 founding NATO Allies. https://t.co/ehxVfXf1RI

The Curtain Falls - With President Truman in attendance, British Prime Minister Winston Churchill delivered his “Iron Curtain” speech in 1946 that outlined how Europe became divided after World War II. https://t.co/iGZKS9RDwq
We are exploring @NATO history through photos. Take a look back at some memorable moments 🧵in the organization’s 75-year history. #NATOSummit

How much do you know about NATO and Major League Baseball? @NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg threw out the ceremonial first pitch at a Washington Nationals game ahead of this week’s #NATOSummit. And we quizzed fans ahead of the game. https://t.co/3I801FZPUd
.@SecBlinken gives remarks to NATO Ally and partner foreign ministers at a #NATOSummit dinner in Washington. https://t.co/5h91LCZW2o

NATO’s commitment to collective defense is enshrined in Article 5 of the Washington Treaty. It ensures every inch of NATO territory on both sides of the Atlantic will be defended if ever attacked by an adversary. https://t.co/R51D61vq6o
.@SecBlinken kicks off the NATO Public Forum in Washington, DC to celebrate 75 years of the Alliance. #NATOSummit https://t.co/ZfP9CBcCJt

From the moment that Putin launched his full-scale invasion, Ukrainian women have stepped up in every conceivable way to defend their country, to defend their communities, to defend their families, including in combat. —  @SecBlinken https://t.co/qzG4hKrtAL
When women are meaningfully involved in making and keeping the peace, it is more likely to endure. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/OzXnpKKVzO

75 years. 32 Allies. 1 Billion people protected.

Born from the ashes of WWII, @NATO is a beacon of peace and stability. #StrongerTogether https://t.co/rFyHYGds3U
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the NATO Women, Peace, and Security Reception at the Department of State. https://t.co/h6pt0UDCei
32 Allies strong and our collective ability to deter and defend has never been greater. https://t.co/tA4J51s6N7
The Washington #NATOSummit gets underway this week. Learn how you can get involved with the Summit as we celebrate 75 years of @NATO: https://t.co/46clpNguDu

At the heart of NATO is a commitment from each Ally to come to the defense of any other Ally. That means that a would-be aggressor knows that if they pick a fight with one, they pick a fight with all. https://t.co/rubXp3lelu

Why should young people care about @NATO? At this year’s NATO Youth Summit in Miami, we asked these future leaders what the Alliance meant to them. Here’s what they had to say. #NATOSummit https://t.co/gS2Xh1i79K

The #NATOSummit with all 32 Allies is finally here. Not familiar with what @NATO has done for you? We break it down, from how the Alliance preserves peace to how products you use every day are a result of Alliance collaborations. https://t.co/MjMEpoMuwm

By expanding the @NATO Alliance, we have fortified the shield against aggression and brought more people under its protection. Together, we are focused on the real work of delivering for our people. https://t.co/dw9JpzJSsb

Independence Day festivities started ahead of July 4th for many of our colleagues serving at U.S. Embassies and Consulates around the world. This year, the United States is celebrating 248 years of independence, freedom, and democracy. https://t.co/GbVLZL99iV

🇺🇸 Our Ambassadors are on the frontlines everyday sharing our American values with the world. For Independence Day, we thought you might be interested to hear how they would describe the U.S. to others, in just a few words. https://t.co/5b0ErPu9rW
Happy Independence Day! Wishing everyone a safe and enjoyable 4th of July. 🎇🇺🇸 🎆 https://t.co/R4YxAYxZgT
We stand with Ukraine as it defends its sovereignty and territory against Russia’s ongoing brutal aggression. https://t.co/fHn0SmR5j2

The Civil Rights Act turns 60 years old today. This 1964 legislation was the culmination of a decades-long movement, led by Martin Luther King Jr., to achieve equality for African-Americans. @ShareAmerica asked experts to reflect on its legacy. https://t.co/56CVYi221h

The power of collective defense is the strongest deterrent to aggression. @NATO is foundational to our security — and we look forward to welcoming the Alliance to DC next week for the #NATOSummit. https://t.co/YyIXhtVJ11
.@SecBlinken participates in a conversation on America’s foreign policy at @BrookingsInst. https://t.co/D940oeOZwB
